maintenance.law: Section 125 CrPC / Section 20 of DV Act, 2005
maintenance.summary: You can claim monthly financial support from your spouse if you are unable to maintain yourself.
steps: Visit the Family Court in your district (or Judicial Magistrate Court if no Family Court exists).
Obtain the maintenance application form from the filing counter.
Attach marriage proof, income details of both parties, and your financial needs statement.
Pay the nominal court fee at the counter and collect your case number.
Attend the first hearing date mentioned on your receipt.
maintenance.timeline: First hearing within 30–60 days; interim maintenance may be granted earlier.
maintenance.fees: Nominal — typically ₹50–₹500 depending on claim amount.

domestic-violence.courtType: Magistrate Court (Protection Officer route also available)
domestic-violence.law: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
domestic-violence.summary: You are protected from physical, emotional, sexual, and economic abuse within the home.
steps: Contact the Protection Officer in your district or dial 181 (Women Helpline) for immediate help.
File an application before the Magistrate under the DV Act.
Describe incidents with dates and attach evidence (medical reports, photos, messages).
Request protection order, residence order, or monetary relief as needed.
Attend the ex-parte or regular hearing as scheduled.
documents: Evidence of abuse (medical reports, FIR copy, photos)
domestic-violence.timeline: Urgent ex-parte orders possible within days; full hearing in 60–90 days.
domestic-violence.fees: No court fee for filing under DV Act.

divorce.law: Hindu Marriage Act, 1955 / Special Marriage Act, 1954
divorce.summary: Legal dissolution of marriage — mutual consent or contested divorce.
steps: Visit the Family Court in your district.
File divorce petition (mutual or contested) with grounds stated.
For mutual consent: both parties must appear; 6-month cooling period may apply.
Attend counselling session if ordered by the court.
Obtain the final decree after all hearings.

dowry.summary: Criminal complaint for dowry demand, harassment, or cruelty.
steps: File an FIR at your local police station under Section 498A IPC.
Keep a copy of the FIR and daily diary (DD) number.
Apply for protection or residence orders under DV Act if needed.
Cooperate with the investigating officer and provide evidence.
Follow up in court when the charge sheet is filed.
documents: List of dowry items and demands
dowry.timeline: FIR immediate; investigation 60–90 days; trial varies.

custody.law: Guardian and Wards Act / Hindu Minority and Guardianship Act
custody.summary: Petition for child custody, visitation rights, or guardianship.
steps: File custody petition in the Family Court of the district where the child resides.
State why you are the better guardian for the child's welfare.
Attend mediation or counselling if ordered.
Comply with interim visitation orders.
Attend final hearing for permanent custody order.
documents: Evidence of your ability to care for the child
custody.timeline: Interim orders in 30–60 days; final order may take 6–12 months.

property.summary: Claim your share in ancestral or joint family property as a daughter or legal heir.
steps: Consult a lawyer or DLSA for free legal aid (call 15100).
File a partition suit in the Civil Court of the district where property is located.
Attach property documents, succession proof, and family tree.
Request interim injunction if property is being sold.
Attend hearings for partition decree.
documents: Succession certificate or legal heir certificate
property.fees: Court fee based on property value — legal aid available.

harassment.law: POSH Act, 2013 (Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace)
harassment.summary: Complaint against sexual harassment at your workplace.
steps: File a written complaint with your employer's Internal Complaints Committee (ICC).
If no ICC or unsatisfied with response, approach the Local Complaints Committee (LCC).
ICC must complete inquiry within 90 days.
If needed, file a case in the Labour Court or civil court for compensation.
Keep copies of all complaints and responses.
documents: Written complaint with incident details and dates
harassment.timeline: ICC inquiry within 90 days; court case timeline varies.

up:lucknow.filingCounter: Ground Floor, New Block — Filing Counter No. 3
up:lucknow.legalAid: DLSA Lucknow — Room 12, District Court Campus

bihar:patna.address: High Court Campus Area, Patna, Bihar — 800001

defaultDistrictInfo.address: District Court Complex — confirm exact address at local registry
defaultDistrictInfo.timings: 10:30 AM – 4:30 PM (Mon–Fri) — may vary by state
defaultDistrictInfo.filingCounter: Ask at the main gate for the Filing Counter
